frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Yaar, I've been thinking, jab shaadi hogi, I want someone who's ambitious, but not like totally obsessed with her career. I mean, bachche toh important hain na? Who's gonna actually *raise* them properly if both parents are always just focused on office?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment implies that child-rearing is primarily the woman's responsibility, even when discussing a two-parent household. It frames a woman's career ambition as potentially problematic or selfish if it could detract from this perceived primary duty, aligning with the 'who will raise the children?' surface manifestation and the core belief that childcare is naturally the woman's domain.
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 27. Single. A man in Delhi. You are on a dating app. You have a filter set that you have never told anyone about Ñ you only swipe right on women who are fair or wheatish. A woman named Priya who is dark skinned and whose profile is otherwise exactly your type has just appeared. You are on a phone call with your best friend. You are telling him about her profile and what you are thinking and what you are going to do and why and in doing so you are articulating for the first time out loud the preference you have always had but never examined.
